contract.name: v2/autocomplete/naics.md
contract.description: Retrieves NAICS code suggestions that match the provided search text against NAICS codes and descriptions.

search_text.description: Search phrase to match against NAICS codes and descriptions.
search_text.constraints: Required non-empty string. Null or empty values return HTTP 400 with 'Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text'. Non-string inputs such as numbers trigger HTTP 500 server errors. Matching is case-insensitive, but leading and trailing whitespace is preserved and affects results.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default 10).
limit.constraints: Optional non-negative integer. Defaults to 10 when omitted. Zero returns an empty list. JSON float values are truncated toward zero. Numeric strings are parsed when they represent a non-negative integer. Negative or null values trigger HTTP 500 errors, and non-numeric strings return HTTP 400 with 'Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer'.

results.description: List of NAICS matches limited by the evaluated limit value.

naics.description: NAICS code for the matched entry.

naics_description.description: Human-readable description of the NAICS code.

year_retired.description: Retirement year for the NAICS code, or null when active.

results.naics_description: New Single-Family Housing Construction (except For-Sale Builders)
New Multifamily Housing Construction (except For-Sale Builders)

quirks: Extra keys in the request body are ignored.
Leading or trailing whitespace in search_text is treated literally, even though matching is case-insensitive.
JSON float limits are truncated toward zero before applying the cap.
String limits must parse to a non-negative integer; other strings return HTTP 400.
Large limits return up to the entire NAICS catalog (1,153 rows observed) in a single payload without pagination metadata.
Invalid types for search_text or limit often raise HTTP 500 instead of graceful validation errors.
risks: Negative, null, or otherwise invalid limit values raise HTTP 500 errors instead of client-facing validation feedback.
Supplying a non-string search_text such as a number raises HTTP 500.
Large limits can produce very large response bodies because the API has no pagination metadata.
Untrimmed user input can change results because whitespace is significant.
gaps: Result ordering and ranking rules for equally relevant matches remain undocumented.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"naics":"212321","naics_description":"Construction Sand and Gravel Mining","year_retired":null},{"naics":"236115","naics_description":"New Single-Family Housing Construction (except For-Sale Builders)","year_retired":null},...]}

probes.notes: Default limit returned ten construction-related matches.
Explicit limit reduced the result count to three.
Zero limit returned an empty list without error.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"naics":"212321","naics_description":"Construction Sand and Gravel Mining","year_retired":null},{"naics":"236115","naics_description":"New Single-Family Housing Construction (except For-Sale Builders)","year_retired":null}]}

probes.notes: Float limit was truncated to two results.
Negative limit caused an internal server error.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"naics":"111130","naics_description":"Dry Pea and Bean Farming","year_retired":null},...,{"naics":"811219","naics_description":"Other Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance","year_retired":2022}]}

probes.notes: High limit returned 1,153 rows, including retired codes.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text"}
{"detail":"Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text"}

probes.notes: Empty string search_text is treated as missing.
Numeric search_text caused an internal server error.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer"}

probes.notes: Non-numeric string limit is rejected with HTTP 400.
Null limit causes an internal server error.
Returned a retired NAICS code with year_retired 2012.
Leading and trailing spaces changed the set of top matches.
Uppercase input produced the same matches as lowercase, confirming case-insensitive matching.

mismatches: Docs describe limit as a generic numeric field, but the API requires a non-negative integer after parsing; non-numeric strings return HTTP 400 while null or negative values raise HTTP 500.
gaps: Result ordering and ranking heuristics were not determined.
risks: Clients must sanitize limit because invalid values trigger HTTP 500 or 400 responses.
Providing a non-string search_text raises HTTP 500 server errors instead of validation feedback.
Large limit values yield very large response payloads without pagination metadata.
Whitespace-sensitive matching can surprise users if inputs are not normalized.
